title: A zero-scale KdV conservative update leaves the state unchanged

source.notes: Basic sanity check for the KdV conservative update.

informal.statement: If the update scale is zero, the KdV conservative update returns the original state value at every grid index.
assumptions: nonlinear and dispersion are real numbers
u is a real-valued grid state
i is a natural-number grid index
informal.expected_conclusion: The zero-scale update is the identity update.

declarations.name: kdvConservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: def kdvConservativeUpdate (scale nonlinear dispersion : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := conservativeUpdate scale u (kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion u) i

declarations.name: kdvConservativeUpdate_zero_scale
declarations.statement: theorem kdvConservativeUpdate_zero_scale (nonlinear dispersion : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : kdvConservativeUpdate 0 nonlinear dispersion u i = u i

tasks.input: Translate the identity property of a zero-scale KdV conservative update into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem kdvConservativeUpdate_zero_scale (nonlinear dispersion : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : kdvConservativeUpdate 0 nonlinear dispersion u i = u i

tasks.input: Given kdvConservativeUpdate and conservativeUpdate_zero_scale, complete the proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding kdvConservativeUpdate and applying conservativeUpdate_zero_scale.

tasks.input: Find the existing LeanNumerics theorem that proves zero-scale conservative updates are identity updates.
